notes: Manual addition: the 'museum ← Muse' etymology, placed in words_language (an etymology hook belongs here, not in mythology — and it avoids colliding with the Mnemosyne/Muses clue in the mythology pack). Grounded in the Museum article's etymology.

questions.clue: This everyday English word for a building of exhibits and collections descends, by way of the Greek mouseion, from a shrine to the nine goddesses of poetry, music, and the arts.

citations.title: Museum
citations.snippet: A museum is an institution dedicated to displaying and preserving culturally or scientifically significant objects. Many museums have exhibitions of these objects on public display, and some have non-exhibited collections that are used by r
